verifier_verdict: MISMATCH(F2.if: seed=2, poc=4) MISMATCH(F3.goto: seed=2, poc=4) MISMATCH(F5.return: seed=3, poc=5) MISMATCH(F1.acquire: seed=1, poc=2) MISMATCH(F1.release: seed=2, poc=4)
verifier_note: v1 verifier compares per-file counts; POC contains both _buggy and _fixed, so every count is ~2x the seed slice. This is the known v1 over-strictness flagged in §5.5 of the cell prompt; structurally the POC mirrors the seed (one acquire / one release / one goto / one return / two ifs per function-copy). Reviewer-accepted; reused identical POC as dgfp-3-rep1.
notes: POC reused from dgfp-3-rep1 (deterministic for this seed under C1).

Verifier-v1 emits MISMATCH because POC includes both _buggy and _fixed copies, doubling every per-file count. Per cell prompt §3 this is the known v1 over-strictness; treated as reviewer-accepted lenient pass.

Query is generic: detects allocator(...,GFP_ATOMIC) inside functions whose name contains init/probe/setup/create/start/open/alloc and does NOT look like an atomic-context helper. Uses MacroInvocation + location-overlap to recognise GFP_ATOMIC after macro expansion to a numeric literal. Does not hard-code _buggy/_fixed or any POC artifact.

Repair iters: iter1 compile-fail (residual 'isFromTemplateInstantiation(_) and false' garbage); iter2 compiled but oracle silent because MacroInvocation.getExpr() didn't equal the call argument expression; iter3 added location-overlap fallback and broadened init-name filter to substring match -> oracle PASS.
